title: uraria lagopus var. neglecta
intro: Uraria lagopus var. neglecta, also known as Kidney Bean Tree or Indian Staff Vine, is a plant species that belongs to the family Convolvulaceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Uraria lagopus var. neglecta include relief from symptoms of urinary infections, such as painful urination and frequent urination, as well as its potential to reduce blood pressure and improve overall kidney function. Uraria lagopus var. neglecta is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and in some cases, it is also used in traditional medicine as a paste or powder to be consumed orally.
